reasoning: The git cherry-pick in prepare() applies a commit from the same trusted repository (github.com/powerline/powerline). Deepseek's source code review found no malicious behavior, and cherry-picking pinned commits is standard in AUR git packages.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen overreacted to the git cherry-pick operation without verifying it's from the same repository. Deepseek's comprehensive source review validated safety.
coverage_issues: Qwen skipped source code review after flagging PKGBUILD, missing critical verification
Neither reviewed .install script execution context (though deemed safe)
false_positives: Qwen's 'untrusted code' claim: cherry-pick uses pinned commit from same repo (6f8da88)
learnings: Improve VCS operation handling: Teach models that same-repo cherry-picks aren't inherently risky
Require continued audit after PKGBUILD flags to validate actual risks in source
Prioritize files executed during build: PKGBUILD > install hooks > source with FS/network access
Enforce reviewing cherry-picked commits when present
